data.title: The 'Reasoning' Upgrade Tesla (TSLA) Promised Is Already Partly Here
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) is beginning to roll out a key upgrade to its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system earlier than expected, according to the company's Head of AI, Ashok Elluswamy. More precisely, he said that some of the "reasoning" features that were supposed to arrive with version 14.3 have already started appearing in the current v14.2 software. It is worth noting that Tesla and CEO Elon Musk have long suggested that reasoning will be a major step forward for FSD, as it will allow the system to think through decisions in a more human-like way.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, during Tesla's Q3 earnings call back in October, Musk explained that this reasoning would allow a car to choose better parking spots, drop passengers off at store entrances, and then park itself more intelligently than a human. He even suggested that by version 14.3, the vehicle would feel as though it were sentient. While that version has not arrived yet, the latest release, v14.2.2.2, is already showing early signs of this shift.

Elluswamy confirmed that reasoning is now being used to get around construction zones and to choose parking spots. He also said that more of these features will roll out during the first quarter. Notably, Tesla had hinted months ago that parking improvements would come with v14.2, but they were initially listed as future updates and had not appeared until now. As a result, Tesla owners should see better routing, smoother navigation, and more comfortable driving overall, especially as more reasoning is added.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 8.4% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: XRP Price Defies Market Gravity as Ripple Gains a Major Regulatory License in London
data.description:
The global fight for crypto legitimacy has just taken a massive leap forward in London. Ripple's UK subsidiary has officially gained approval from the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) to act as an Electronic Money Institution. This move allows the company to issue electronic money and handle payment services across the country. For many investors, this news acts as a powerful catalyst for the XRP price (XRP-USD), proving that the digital asset is moving closer to mainstream financial adoption in one of the world's most important money centers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It's a Masterstroke for Ripple's Long Term Survival

Securing this EMI registration is a strategic masterstroke for Ripple's long-term survival. The new license is particularly important as the company prepares to roll out its own stablecoin, Ripple USD.

Driving this expansion is a clear plan to use the UK as a base for stable, regulated payment flows that bypass the drama of the U.S. legal system. This regulatory shield helps stabilize the XRP price by showing that the technology is being embraced by top-tier government watchdogs.

UK Authorities Are Still Moving with Caution

While the approval is a massive win, the UK authorities are still moving with caution. Ripple Markets UK currently faces some limits, such as a ban on offering services directly to everyday retail customers or running crypto ATMs without extra permission. The firm is building its reputation through these strict rules to ensure it can apply for even broader powers by the October 2027 deadline. These temporary guardrails help prevent the kind of volatility that usually hurts the XRP price, replacing wild speculation with a slow and steady climb toward institutional trust.

Ripple Rejects IPO Noise

This London victory comes at a time when Ripple is doubling down on its identity as a private powerhouse. Despite a recent fundraise that pushed the company's valuation to a staggering $40 billion, leadership has confirmed they have no plans to go public anytime soon. By staying private, the company can focus entirely on its technology and the expansion of the XRP Ledger without the pressure of quarterly stock reports.

As Ripple secures more of these global licenses, the XRP price remains the primary way for the market to measure the success of this growing digital empire. At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $2.1352.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why OKLO Stock Is Soaring Today
data.description:
Shares of U.S.-based Oklo Inc. (OKLO) surged more than 18% in early trading today after Meta Platforms (META) announced deals with three nuclear power providers. As Meta works to secure energy for its growing AI projects, nuclear stocks surged, with Oklo and other energy companies posting strong gains. Vistra Corp. (VST) also rose 17% in early trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Oklo develops compact nuclear microreactors aimed at providing reliable, low-carbon power for commercial and industrial use.

Meta Signs Nuclear Energy Deals

Meta has teamed up with Vistra, TerraPower, and Oklo—all working in nuclear power—to supply energy for its Prometheus supercluster, currently under construction at a data center in New Albany, Ohio. The company said that its collaboration with the three nuclear firms could add 6.6 gigawatts of power by 2035, enough to meet the entire energy demand of New Hampshire.

To be precise, Meta plans to support several small nuclear reactors being developed by Oklo over the next decade.

The deals are the latest step in Meta's push to secure the energy needed for its AI infrastructure as the company works toward Mark Zuckerberg's goal of building superintelligent AI.

What Meta's Deal Means for OKLO

While Meta didn't reveal the contract values, deals of this size could bring billions of dollars in revenue to electricity providers. Being selected by a Big Tech company like Meta also boosts Oklo's credibility, making it easier to attract investors, partners, and customers.

Additionally, the partnership gives Oklo steady demand and support for its small nuclear reactors, which could help speed up construction and production. Meta noted that Oklo's advanced nuclear technology campus in Pike County, Ohio, is expected to be operational by 2030.

Overall, the deal positions Oklo as a leading player in next-generation nuclear energy, potentially opening opportunities for more large-scale energy contracts in the future.

Is OKLO a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on OKLO stock, based on eight Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. The average share price target for Oklo is $130.10, which implies an upside of 33.3% from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Bitcoin Refuses to Budge at $92k as the Supreme Court Prepares to Strike a Blow to Trade Tariffs
data.description:
The world's largest cryptocurrency is refusing to budge. On Friday, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) remained glued to the $90,000-$92,000 level. This has left traders in a state of no trade limbo. The entire financial world is currently fixated on the U.S. Supreme Court, which is expected to rule on the legality of controversial trade tariffs.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Because these tariffs have such a huge impact on global inflation and how much cash investors are willing to risk, Bitcoin has become a secondary thought until a verdict is reached. For now, the start of the year chop continues to keep the market in a tight, frustrating range.

The Federal Reserve Is Dealing with a Cooling Labour market

While the courts are busy with trade policy, the Federal Reserve is dealing with a cooling labor market. Recent unemployment data came in lower than many expected, giving the Fed a reason to keep interest rates exactly where they are for the January meeting. Driving this cautious approach is a clear sign that the economy is slowing down. This has made officials hesitant to continue cutting rates just yet. At the heart of the current market boredom lies a Fed that is effectively in "wait and see" mode, which has stripped the crypto market of the explosive volatility it usually sees in early January.

Technical Signals Guard the $90,000 Floor

From a technical perspective, Bitcoin is fighting to stay above its 21-day moving average. If it fails to hold this ground, analysts warn of a potential "cascade" that could send prices tumbling toward the $88,000 mark. On the flip side, a breakout above $92,000 would be needed to prove that the bulls are still in charge. The market is currently finding support in a critical order block where big buyers are absorbing any sell pressure that hits the screen. Until the Supreme Court delivers a definitive yes or no on the tariff issue, these technical lines are the only map traders have left to follow.

Markets Sprint Toward a Breakout Catalyst

The lack of movement today doesn't mean the market is dead; it just means it is coiled like a spring. Traders like Daan Crypto Trades have noted that they aren't interested in chasing small 5% moves while the price is trapped in this two-month range.

Overall, everyone is looking for a decisive breakout that will set the tone for the rest of 2026. Whether that catalyst comes from the Supreme Court ruling or a shift in the Fed's tone later this month, the ghost of the $90,000 deadlock will eventually have to give way to a new trend.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $91,334.87.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Netflix Is 'Not a Screaming Buy' despite Selloff, Says Investor
data.description:
Shares of Netflix (NFLX) have fallen sharply since October, after the video streaming platform emerged as a leading bidder for entertainment rival Warner Bros. Discovery (DIS). The stock is down about 28% in less than three months and roughly one-third from its June 30 peak, yet some investors still believe it is not cheap enough to step in. For example, Christopher Brown of Synovus Securities told Bloomberg that Netflix is "not a screaming buy" at current levels, even though he personally owns the stock and it is held in Synovus portfolios.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Indeed, even after the selloff, Netflix is trading at about 28 times forward earnings, which is higher than rivals like Walt Disney, Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Paramount Skydance (PSKY). Still, Netflix's valuation is below its own five-year average multiple of 34, which makes the stock look cheaper than it has historically. However, uncertainty around the Warner Bros deal, valued at $82.7 billion, has become a major overhang, as investors worry about cost, regulatory risk, and Netflix's limited experience with large acquisitions.

Those concerns have made Netflix one of the worst performers in the Nasdaq 100 since late June, and some analysts remain cautious. For instance, Wedbush's Joel Kulina told Bloomberg that the deal could weigh on the stock for months. Meanwhile, CFRA recently downgraded Netflix to a Hold rating due to the risks tied to Warner Bros.' high debt and the shift away from Netflix's long-standing strategy of avoiding big acquisitions.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $130.17 per share implies 46.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Tariff Case Won't Receive a Supreme Court Ruling on Friday
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court won't issue a ruling on the legality of President Trump's tariffs today, according to Reuters. Earlier this week, the nation's highest court announced that it would meet for opinion day at 10 a.m. on Friday. SCOTUS doesn't provide advance notice of which decisions it will release, although investors speculated that the case could receive expedited treatment due to its significance.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The court heard oral arguments for the case last November, with some justices expressing skepticism that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) provides legal authority for the tariffs. The IEEPA allows the president to enforce tariffs in response to national emergencies.

Trump Team Eyes Backup Tariff Plans

Earlier today, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said the Trump administration is considering other legal avenues to impose tariffs if the court issues an unfavorable outcome. "Our expectation is that ⁠we're going to ​win, and if we don't win, then we know that we've got other tools ​that we could use that get us to the same place," Hassett told CNBC.

Potential backup plans include Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act, which allow the president to impose tariffs or trade restrictions to address unfair trade practices or protect domestic industries.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Morgan Stanley (MS) Sees Upside in Gold After Venezuela Attack
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Morgan Stanley (MS) expects the price of gold to march higher after the U.S. attacked Venezuela and captured President Nicolás Maduro.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a report assessing the market implications of the U.S. military action in Venezuela, Morgan Stanley writes that "uncertainty brings upside" for the price of gold, referring to the precious metal's role as a safe-haven asset in times of geopolitical strife.

"The uncertainty about what's next in U.S.-Venezuela relations keeps the attractiveness of gold as a safe haven. Additionally, any weakness of the U.S. dollar could give another boost to gold," writes Morgan Stanley in a wide-ranging forecast for financial markets after the U.S. deposed Maduro.

Forecast for Gold's Price

Morgan Stanley currently forecasts that the price of gold will reach $4,800 an ounce by the end of 2026. That's about 6% higher than where gold currently trades and is among the more conservative outlooks on Wall Street. Several investment banks and brokerages see gold's price topping $5,000 an ounce this year.

Morgan Stanley's latest outlook comes as precious metals enjoy a bull market. The price of gold rose more than 60% in 2025 to climb above $4,000 an ounce for the first time, driven largely by increased purchases among the world's central banks. At the same time, the price of silver more than doubled last year and crossed above $80 an ounce for the first time on record.

Is MS Stock a Buy?

Morgan Stanley's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 14 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on seven Buy and seven Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MS price target of $185 implies 0.63% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: QSR, MCD, CMG: A Total of 4,000 Restaurants in Canada Forecast to Shutdown in 2026
data.description:
Things appear to be going from bad to worse with Canada's economy, impacting the food services industry.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Persistent inflation, rising food prices, and U.S. tariffs are conspiring to slow Canada's economy and clobber the country's restaurant industry. A new study has found that about 7,000 restaurants, diners, and pubs shut down in 2025, and another 4,000 are forecast to close their doors this year.

The study by Dalhousie University in Halifax, Nova Scotia says that Canadian consumers are pulling back on discretionary spending and eating out less often. At the same time, Canadians are becoming pickier about where they eat and more frugal with their food budgets.

Implications for Restaurants in Canada

While the majority of restaurants shutting down are independently owned, the situation in Canada has implications for publicly traded restaurant chains that operate in the country. From homegrown companies such as Restaurant Brands International (QSR), which owns the Burger King hamburger and Popeyes chicken chains, to Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), which is expanding across Canada.

Canada is also a major market for restaurant chains such as McDonald's (MCD) and Starbucks (SBUX). In fact, Starbucks announced last fall that it is closing 50 locations throughout Canada, mostly in the major cities of Toronto and Vancouver, due to slumping sales and chronic underperformance.

News of the widespread restaurant closures comes as Canada's unemployment rate rose 0.3 percentage points to 6.8% in December as the country's labor market deteriorates and more people look for work.

Is QSR Stock a Buy?

Restaurant Brands International's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, eight Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average QSR price target of $76.57 implies 12.50% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Chinese Humanoid Robots Leaving U.S. Rivals Tesla and Nvidia in the Dust
data.description:
China is winning the humanoid robot wars striding past their U.S. rivals such as Tesla (TSLA) and Nvidia (NVDA) in shipments, price and sales.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to new data from Omdia, Chinese humanoid robot makers dominated the market in 2025, with five firms in the top 10, and it could be the same pattern in 2026.

Thousands of Humanoids

Market research firm Omdia found that start-up Shanghai AgiBot InnovationTechnology was top of the robot class shipping 5,168 humanoids last year. That amounts to around 40% of the overall market.

Unitree Robotics, whose models are well-versed in winning sporting medals for their athleticism, sprinted to second spot with 4,200 humanoids. That accounted for a third of the overall market. UBTech was third with annual shipments of 1,000 units.

In its report, General-Purpose Embodied Robotics Market Radar, Omdia said the global humanoid robot market entered a phase of rapid growth in 2025, with total shipments expected to reach about 13,000 units for the year.

Humanoid robots are built and designed to look like and move like human beings. They are powered by AI algorithms and complex hardware like semiconductors. They could be used in a whole range of settings from the battlefield, to the factory floor, in hospital surgeries or dusting away the cobwebs in your living room.

U.S. Robots Yet to Fire

Global sales more than quintupled from 2024, the figures said, highlighting interest in what some refer to as "physical AI", as well as the ability of Chinese companies to produce such robots at industrial scale.

"Chinese vendors are setting benchmarks in large-scale production," Omdia said.

U.S firms are still very much in the early stage of development such as Tesla's Optimus, which is set to begin mass production in 2026. Last year, Nvidia launched the Isaac GR00T N1, the world's first open, fully customizable foundation model for generalized humanoid reasoning and skills. It can grasp, move objects with one or both arms, and transfer items from one arm to another.
data.author: David Craik
